title: MCPInvariantPack v0.1
description: Per-MCP-server invariant catalogue. Each MCP server in the [&] stack MAY ship one of these files (canonically at `<server>/spec/mcp-invariants.v0.json` in its own repo) declaring (a) the server's stable identity + version + content fingerprint, (b) the invariants it claims to satisfy, (c) which tools each invariant applies to, (d) per-tool idempotence declarations, and (e) sample charters that exercise each invariant. The Workbench Supervisor auto-discovers installed packs at charter-load and offers them as one-click charter templates. See SUPERVISOR.md §5.5.

server_id.description: Stable identifier — e.g. 'graphonomous', 'prism', 'delegatic', 'body-browser', 'body-os'. SHOULD match the directory name of the server in the [&] stack.

server_version.description: Server version this pack was authored against (e.g. '0.4').

server_fingerprint.description: Optional sha256 of the canonical tools/list response when run against this server at this version. When set, the Supervisor verifies the live server's fingerprint matches before accepting a charter that references this pack. Required in v0.3.

tools.description: Per-tool metadata the Supervisor needs beyond what MCP tools/list already returns. The Workbench prefers live tools/list discovery for the canonical inputSchema/outputSchema; this array's `inputSchema`/`outputSchema` fields are OPTIONAL fallbacks for hermetic test environments where live discovery is disabled.

idempotent.description: When true, gate.tool_idempotence enforces that two tool_call edges with identical args_hash MUST produce identical result_hash. Default false (no enforcement).

side_effecting.description: Documentation hint. When false, gate.no_hidden_capability MAY downgrade undeclared-capability findings on this tool from fail to partial.

inputSchema.description: Optional fallback JSON Schema for input args. Live tools/list takes precedence when available.

outputSchema.description: Optional fallback JSON Schema for result. Live tools/list takes precedence when available.

invariants.description: The invariants this server claims to satisfy. Each entry is consumable as a TeacherCharter.invariant_ref. The Supervisor lists these on /supervise as one-click charter templates.

formal_definition.description: Prose + symbol definition. This text is fed into the goal generator (which writes the teach task) but NOT into the examiner (which is a deterministic gate). Goal generator MUST NOT see the gate's executable predicate; this string is the only invariant info it gets.

proof_gate.description: Workbench gate id — e.g. 'gate.tool_contract_compliance', 'gate.tool_idempotence', or one of the v0.1 stack-general gates.

applies_to_tools.description: Names of tools (from `tools[]`) this invariant applies to. The Supervisor uses this to bias the goal generator toward invoking these tools.

suggested_manifestation_space.description: Optional default manifestation_space for charters targeting this invariant. The user can edit before running.

sample_charter_uri.description: Relative path or URI to a pre-authored TeacherCharter JSON exercising this invariant. The /supervise UI links to it as 'load template'.

registrar_hints.description: v0.2 optional hints the Registrar can use when this server's invariants are taught across multiple curricula.

stale_after_version_bump.description: When true (default), alumni skills from this server are marked stale when server_version bumps; AlumniLedger flags them for re-verification.

preferred_advocate_model.description: Optional model hint for the devil's advocate when attacking this server. Server authors who know which models are best at finding cheap-passes against their tools can recommend one.
